jokes.text: 'Hey, Max! Is this your baby sister? What a cute, little girl!'
jokes.setup: Liz mistakes an adult man for a child
jokes.punchline: The awkward assumption and gendering
jokes.explanation: Sets up the central romantic plot while establishing Liz's baby obsession

jokes.text: 'Or boy, if you grow up and feel that's what's inside you'
jokes.setup: Already awkward misgendering of adult as child
jokes.punchline: Doubling down with progressive gender philosophy to a grown man
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly escalates the awkwardness while satirizing liberal guilt

jokes.text: 'O-M-G-, Liz. Look at you and me and our biological clocks'
jokes.setup: Traditional discussion of female biological clocks
jokes.punchline: Next line reveals Jenna's 'clock' is getting turned on by car accidents
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the dark punchline that follows

jokes.text: 'You're going baby crazy and I keep getting turned on by car accidents'
jokes.setup: Comparison of biological clocks
jokes.punchline: Jenna's version is disturbing sexual fetish
jokes.explanation: Dark, unexpected, perfectly in character for Jenna. Excellent shock value with character truth

jokes.text: 'It isn't stealing if it fell on the ground'
jokes.setup: Question about where Liz got a baby shoe
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous justification for theft
jokes.explanation: Shows Liz's baby obsession driving her to minor crimes with flimsy logic

jokes.text: 'When she's in a foul mood, it can be horribly unpleasant. But, at other times, she is asleep'
jokes.setup: Warning about caring for difficult elderly mother
jokes.punchline: Only good times are when she's unconscious
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure and economy, classic Jack deadpan delivery

jokes.text: 'Do you know what kind of clothes old Puerto Rican ladies wear around the house? No. Me neither, because she never wore any'
jokes.setup: Question about cultural clothing traditions
jokes.punchline: Grandmother was a nudist
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with cultural/family humor

jokes.text: 'Jack?! Somebody's got to dry me off!'
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his mother's care to new nurse
jokes.punchline: Mother interrupts naked, demanding help
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy interruption, embarrassing timing

jokes.text: 'Well, you bought three hours of network prime time for your salute to Benny Hill'
jokes.setup: Example of Tracy's out-of-control spending
jokes.punchline: Bought massive TV time for obscure tribute
jokes.explanation: Specific absurdity that fits Tracy's character and celebrity excess

jokes.text: 'I have to spend it all on useless things, like gold shoes and Grizz and Dotcom'
jokes.setup: Explaining need to spend money before divorce
jokes.punchline: Casually refers to his human employees as 'useless things'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about objectifying people, perfectly fits Tracy's oblivious wealth

jokes.text: 'When Angie and I got married, my only assets were a toaster oven and two tickets to a Young M.C. concert'
jokes.setup: Explaining lack of pre-nup
jokes.punchline: Specific dated reference to modest past
jokes.explanation: Good specific details that ground Tracy's backstory

jokes.text: 'I'll bring Angie in. But I'm going to tell her this is all your idea'
jokes.setup: Agreement to try post-nup solution
jokes.punchline: Immediately plans to throw Jack under the bus
jokes.explanation: Shows Tracy's cowardice and self-preservation

jokes.text: 'Don't help me! I'm too proud'
jokes.setup: Liz struggling with something physical
jokes.punchline: Declaring pride while clearly needing help
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Liz's stubborn nature

jokes.text: 'I had to do something to get your attention'
jokes.setup: Stuart asks why she touched his head
jokes.punchline: Weak justification for bizarre behavior
jokes.explanation: Continues the awkward meet-cute setup

jokes.text: 'Howdy, Jack! It's me, Janis Joplin! And I want to audition for my Sheinhardt-Universal biopic so bad that I came here dressed as me. Well, actually... I am me. So... Well, I dressed normal'
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to stay in Janis Joplin character
jokes.punchline: Logic completely falls apart as she realizes she's dressed as herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - theatrical ambition meets complete confusion

jokes.text: 'It's like how we're including a Heroes DVD with every missile guidance system we sell'
jokes.setup: Explaining corporate synergy
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions selling weapons with TV shows
jokes.explanation: Dark corporate satire delivered with perfect casualness

jokes.text: 'Whoa! What is that iron bird? They had airplanes in the '60s, Jenna. Oh, right'
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to stay in 1960s character
jokes.punchline: Doesn't know basic historical facts about airplanes
jokes.explanation: Shows Jenna's historical ignorance and commitment to bad acting

jokes.text: 'It must be like working in the Galactic Senate in Star Wars'
jokes.setup: Comparing UN to entertainment
jokes.punchline: Nerdy Star Wars reference to describe serious international organization
jokes.explanation: Shows Liz's nerd tendencies and pop culture lens

jokes.text: 'We are also very concerned about the growing influence of the Sith Lords'
jokes.setup: Comparison of UN to Star Wars
jokes.punchline: Plays along completely straight with fictional threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan commitment to absurd premise

jokes.text: 'Only insofar as we met because I touched his head thinking he was a child'
jokes.setup: Jenna asking if interest in Stuart is part of mommy thing
jokes.punchline: Admits to extremely awkward origin story
jokes.explanation: Continues the cringe thread while advancing plot

jokes.text: 'Like order a tall coffee or talk about my Nintendo Wii'
jokes.setup: Worrying about saying something stupid on date
jokes.punchline: Examples are innocuous things she thinks are embarrassing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz neurosis about normal activities being shameful

jokes.text: 'Take a lesson from Janis and show some self-control. How far into that biography are you? Not very. Why? What happens?'
jokes.setup: Jenna suggesting Janis Joplin as role model for self-control
jokes.punchline: Admits she hasn't read enough to know Joplin died of overdose
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - suggests self-control icon without knowing the tragic end

jokes.text: 'Or any female of equivalent thickness'
jokes.setup: Post-nup clause about Tracy running away with Hairspray actress
jokes.punchline: Reduces women to weight measurements in legal document
jokes.explanation: Dark corporate humor reducing people to physical specifications

jokes.text: 'I'm going to watch you die, Tracy Jordan'
jokes.setup: Romantic declaration about staying together
jokes.punchline: Morbid promise delivered as romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Darkly romantic threat delivered with genuine affection

jokes.text: 'She's done it before, Jack'
jokes.setup: Angie's death-watching promise
jokes.punchline: Casual confirmation she has literally watched people die
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with maximum economy - four words that are deeply disturbing

jokes.text: 'Nice dress. Are you going to dinner? Don't forget your book'
jokes.setup: Noticing Liz dressed up
jokes.punchline: Assumes she's dining alone with reading material
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's assumptions about Liz's social life

jokes.text: 'Under the bridge'
jokes.setup: Stuart mentions new apartment in Williamsburg
jokes.punchline: Literally lives under a bridge
jokes.explanation: Simple sight gag about NYC real estate costs

jokes.text: 'The U.N.? They still have that? I could have sworn they turned that building into a Barnes and Noble'
jokes.setup: Learning Stuart works at the UN
jokes.punchline: Dismissive assumption about irrelevant institution
jokes.explanation: Good Jack arrogance about international institutions

jokes.text: 'Have you learned how to say Do you want fries with that in Chinese?'
jokes.setup: Criticism of corporate America
jokes.punchline: Specific dig about economic decline and fast food
jokes.explanation: Standard economic anxiety joke

jokes.text: 'Senior V.P. for Television and Microwave Oven Programming'
jokes.setup: Jack introducing his job title
jokes.punchline: Combines prestigious TV with mundane appliances
jokes.explanation: Perfect corporate absurdity - important-sounding but ridiculous

jokes.text: 'U.N. High Commission on Water Temperature and Food Taint'
jokes.setup: Competing with Jack's ridiculous job title
jokes.punchline: Equally absurd UN position with gross-sounding responsibilities
jokes.explanation: Good matching absurdity with unfortunately named department

jokes.text: 'Yeah, you know me. Spend my lunch hour walking up and down Sixth Avenue... looking for a hot meal'
jokes.setup: Stuart saying Liz 'went for it' when they met
jokes.punchline: Liz sarcastically portraying herself as predatory
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor about her awkward approach

jokes.text: 'Classic Lemon man-eater. Cat sound'
jokes.setup: Liz's sarcastic self-description
jokes.punchline: Jack ironically agreeing with obviously false characterization
jokes.explanation: Jack playing along with absurd characterization of socially awkward Liz

jokes.text: 'When I'm on my death bed, Frenching my wife, I will think of you'
jokes.setup: Tracy thanking Jack for marriage advice
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual gratitude involving death
jokes.explanation: Disturbing and hilarious - combines death, sex, and gratitude inappropriately

jokes.text: 'Julia Roberts has agreed to play the tragic singer... Billy in a film directed by Martin Scorsese and written by the best screenwriter in the world. Whoever that is'
jokes.setup: News about competing Janis Joplin movie
jokes.punchline: Wrong name 'Billy' and dismissive unknown writer credit
jokes.explanation: Simple name error and media dismissiveness

jokes.text: 'She fought me at first. But I find that authoritative, rapid Spanish subdues white people'
jokes.setup: How she got Jack's mother to sleep
jokes.punchline: Racial stereotype delivered as professional technique
jokes.explanation: Sharp racial observation delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: 'My nephew just joined the Marines to try to pay for college, but... that's fun'
jokes.setup: Commenting on Tracy's gold shoes gift
jokes.punchline: Contrasts military service for education with frivolous wealth
jokes.explanation: Cutting social commentary on wealth disparity

jokes.text: 'Without getting into specifics, my exit involves a McFlurry machine and a videotape of risqué commercials from overseas'
jokes.setup: Discussing plans for dying alone
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific suicide method involving fast food and foreign ads
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and disturbing suicide plan that's completely unexpected

jokes.text: 'Except that in Puerto Rico, a McFlurry is called a Señor Flurry'
jokes.setup: Jack's elaborate suicide plan involving McFlurry
jokes.punchline: Focuses on incorrect cultural translation detail
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Jack's disturbing plan, focusing on trivial detail

jokes.text: 'Are you trying to pick me up to keep me from touching fire?'
jokes.setup: Liz's protective gesture
jokes.punchline: Treats normal protection as condescending due to height
jokes.explanation: Continues the height sensitivity thread

jokes.text: 'Kofi said you were too good to be true. Not ah told me to wait three days before I called you'
jokes.setup: Stuart talking about his friends' advice
jokes.punchline: Name-drops UN Secretary-General and other diplomats as dating advisors
jokes.explanation: Excellent character detail - world leaders giving relationship advice

jokes.text: 'Instead of, uh... any of it... she'll have a cup of hot water with a chicken bone in it and a bowl of salted ice cubes'
jokes.setup: Liz ordering at fancy restaurant
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific weird dietary restrictions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz food neurosis - specific and disgusting

jokes.text: 'I have something on my ball'
jokes.setup: Offered towel at country club
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of golf ball vs testicle
jokes.explanation: Simple double entendre about his medical concern

jokes.text: I have something on my ball.
jokes.setup: Jack is at what appears to be a golf course
jokes.punchline: Double meaning between golf ball and testicle
jokes.explanation: Simple double entendre that works in context. The economy is excellent but the joke itself is fairly obvious.

jokes.text: 'I didn't know Michael Bublé had that many albums'
jokes.setup: Commenting on Jack's embarrassing possessions
jokes.punchline: Specific musician known for bland easy listening
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation about Jack's secret musical tastes

jokes.text: 'You forgot to put on your underpants'
jokes.setup: Medical examination getting intense
jokes.punchline: Jack is pantsless during conversation
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy embarrassment

jokes.text: 'You should work for The Huffington Post'
jokes.setup: Elisa stating obvious medical advice
jokes.punchline: Insult comparing her to low-quality online journalism
jokes.explanation: Timely media criticism as personal insult

jokes.text: 'Don't try to be Señor Macho Solo'
jokes.setup: Jack trying to handle medical issue alone
jokes.punchline: Spanish version of 'macho man flying solo'
jokes.explanation: Title reference, good bilingual wordplay

jokes.text: 'Which is actually what we call a McRib sandwich'
jokes.setup: Using phrase 'Señor Macho Solo'
jokes.punchline: Claims it's Puerto Rican name for McDonald's item
jokes.explanation: Continues running gag of fake Spanish food names

jokes.text: Which is actually what we call a 'McRib' sandwich.
jokes.setup: Elisa just used the phrase 'Señor Macho Solo'
jokes.punchline: She claims it's also a McDonald's sandwich name
jokes.explanation: Running gag of Puerto Rican McDonald's translations. Less effective than the first instance but maintains the pattern.

jokes.text: 'Why? Do you want to get married? I'll do it'
jokes.setup: Jack asking about needing a soul mate
jokes.punchline: Immediate marriage proposal from Jenna
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna desperation and opportunism

jokes.text: 'Yes, I'm talking. Uh, no, we do not have the music'
jokes.setup: Jenna asking if they have music rights
jokes.punchline: Jack parsing 'yes and no' literally to avoid bad news
jokes.explanation: Good corporate speak evasion technique

jokes.text: 'Socially inept and baby crazy. I feel like I'm in a beer commercial'
jokes.setup: Describing herself to Stuart on phone
jokes.punchline: Compares herself to stereotypical undesirable woman
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness and media reference

jokes.text: Socially inept and baby crazy. I feel like I'm in a beer commercial.
jokes.setup: Liz describes herself as socially inept and baby crazy
jokes.punchline: Stuart compares it to beer commercial demographics
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about dating demographics and advertising. The specificity of beer commercials (vs. all advertising) makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 'Once again, Liz, I am an adult man'
jokes.setup: Liz asking if he saw Sex and the City movie
jokes.punchline: Deadpan reminder of his gender and maturity
jokes.explanation: Good callback to ongoing theme with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: 'Because I'd like-a to make-a the prank phone call'
jokes.setup: Asking to be transferred to Italian ambassador
jokes.punchline: Admits to making ethnic prank calls from UN
jokes.explanation: Unexpected unprofessional behavior from diplomat

jokes.text: 'I brought a '65 Moët and some Pizza-Blasted Pringles'
jokes.setup: Jack bringing gifts to Puerto Rican party
jokes.punchline: Combines expensive champagne with junk food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack class confusion - expensive taste meets processed food

jokes.text: Oh. You were not being rude. You were just being stupid.
jokes.setup: Jack apologizes for being rude earlier
jokes.punchline: Elisa bluntly corrects his self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that's both insulting and endearing. The economy is exceptional - maximum impact with minimum words.

jokes.text: 'Why are they smiling so much? Who's being ostracized?'
jokes.setup: Jack observing happy family gathering
jokes.punchline: Assumes happiness requires exclusion of someone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight - Jack can't comprehend genuine community joy

jokes.text: 'A Blaffair to Rememblack'
jokes.setup: Mentioning movie remakes
jokes.punchline: Fake blaxploitation version title
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau suggesting Jenna's questionable career choices

jokes.text: 'Surround yourself with people and love and babies with pierced ears'
jokes.setup: Giving advice about not being alone
jokes.punchline: Includes specific weird detail about infant body modification
jokes.explanation: Touching sentiment ruined by bizarre specific detail

jokes.text: 'It's exactly like a snow-cone, except they call it a piragua'
jokes.setup: Jack enthusiastically describing Puerto Rican food
jokes.punchline: Describes identical food with different name as exotic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack cultural obliviousness - excited about the same thing with Spanish name

jokes.text: 'The test results were negative. Oh, I see your confusion! That is funny!'
jokes.setup: Medical test results causing confusion
jokes.punchline: Negative results are good news, creating verbal confusion
jokes.explanation: Standard medical results wordplay

jokes.text: 'Once again, I apologize that our regular warm-up comic O.D.'d at a gay man's apartment this morning'
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's doing comedy instead
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions drug death with unnecessary detail
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and specific - Kenneth's innocent delivery of horrible news

jokes.text: 'Can two people fall in love over a benign gonad cyst?'
jokes.setup: Jack questioning his feelings for Elisa
jokes.punchline: Romantic doubt phrased in clinical medical terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect blend of romantic vulnerability with medical terminology

jokes.text: 'Ladies and gentlemen, Miss Janet Jopler!'
jokes.setup: Introducing Jenna's performance
jokes.punchline: Wrong version of Janis Joplin's name
jokes.explanation: Simple name mispronunciation gag

jokes.text: 'Also, uh, we didn't get the song. I wrote this myself five minutes ago'
jokes.setup: Jenna performing on live TV
jokes.punchline: Admits to last-minute amateur songwriting
jokes.explanation: Good corporate panic and corner-cutting

jokes.text: 'Oh, I'm so sorry! I thought he was someone else!'
jokes.setup: Liz approaches wrong person at bridge
jokes.punchline: Repeats the head-touching mistake with stranger
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback that shows Liz hasn't learned
